[Project README: /home/ishanp/Documents/GitHub/igs-mcp/README.md]
# IGS MCP Server

**Intelligence Gathering System** — Local-only MCP server for real-time news monitoring and academic research.

[![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/igs-mcp-server)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/igs-mcp-server)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/ishan-parihar/igs-mcp)](https://github.com/ishan-parihar/igs-mcp/blob/main/LICENSE)

---

## 🎯 What It Does

IGS is an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that provides AI assistants with:

1. **News Monitoring** — Fetch from 223 curated RSS/HTTP sources (Reuters, BBC, Indian Express, etc.)
2. **Reddit Search** — Search any subreddit with time/sort filters
3. **Academic Research** — Search arXiv + Semantic Scholar papers with PDF extraction
4. **Historical Queries** — Access news from specific date ranges via Google News

All processing happens **locally on your machine**. No data is sent to external servers except the news sources themselves.

---

## 🚀 Quick Start

### 1. Install & Configure (Recommended)

```bash
git clone https://github.com/ishan-parihar/igs-mcp.git
cd igs-mcp
./setup.sh
```

The setup script checks prerequisites (Node.js v20+), installs dependencies, builds, bootstraps config files, and prints your Claude Desktop config at the end — ready to copy-paste.

### Manual Install

```bash
git clone https://github.com/ishan-parihar/igs-mcp.git
cd igs-mcp
npm install
npm run build
```

### 2. Configure Claude Desktop

Edit `claude_desktop_config.json`:

**macOS:** `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`  
**Windows:** `%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json`  
**Linux:** `~/.config/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "igs": {
      "command": "node",
      "args": ["/absolute/path/to/igs-mcp/dist/server.js"]
    }
  }
}
```

### 3. Restart Claude Desktop

Completely quit and relaunch Claude Desktop.

---

## 📚 Documentation

| Document | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [INSTALL.md](INSTALL.md) | Complete installation guide |
| [AUDIT_REPORT.md](AUDIT_REPORT.md) | Installation audit & verification |
| `docs/plans/` | Architecture & design documents |

---

## 🛠️ Available Tools

### News Monitoring

| Tool | Description | Example |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `news.fetch` | Fetch from curated sources | `news.fetch(pools=['GLOBAL_BREAKING'], keywords=['AI'], limit=10)` |
| `news.testSource` | Test single source | `news.testSource(id='reuters')` |

### Research Tools

| Tool | Description | Example |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `reddit.search` | Search Reddit | `reddit.search(query='technology', subreddits=['news'], limit=10)` |
| `research.search` | Search papers | `research.search(query='transformer', sources=['arxiv'], limit=10)` |
| `research.paper` | Paper details + PDF | `research.paper(paperId='arxiv:2401.12345', extractPDF=true)` |

### Source Management

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `sources.list` | List all sources |
| `sources.upsert` | Add/update source |
| `sources.delete` | Remove source |
| `sources.countries` | Countries with source counts |
| `sources.cities` | Cities with source counts |
| `sources.domains` | Topic domains |

### Pool Management

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `pools.list` | List all pools |
| `pools.upsert` | Create/update pool |
| `pools.delete` | Delete pool |

---

## 📊 Pre-configured Content

### News Pools (14)

| Pool | Description | Sources |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `GLOBAL_BREAKING` | Fast breaking news | Reuters, BBC, FT, Al Jazeera |
| `GLOBAL_GEOECON` | Geopolitics & geoeconomics | Foreign Affairs, RAND, Brookings |
| `GLOBAL_LAW_REG` | Law & regulation | OFAC, Lawfare, SCOTUSblog |
| `GLOBAL_TECH_CYBER` | Tech & cyber | MIT Tech Review, Ars Technica, CISA |
| `GLOBAL_ENV_HEALTH` | Environment & health | WHO, Mongabay, Nature |
| `GLOBAL_CULT_SOC` | Culture & society | Aeon, UnHerd, Noema |
| `INDIA_NATIONAL_BASE` | India national | The Hindu, Indian Express, Economic Times |
| `INDIA_WATCHDOG` | India investigative | Scroll.in, The Wire, Article 14 |
| `INDIA_FACTCHECK_DATA` | India fact-check | Alt News, BOOM Live, IndiaSpend |
| `INDIA_BUSINESS_REG` | India business | Moneycontrol, NSE, RBI, SEBI |
| `INDIA_REGION` | India regional | IE South/East/Northeast |
| `INDIA_CITIES` | India cities | Delhi, Mumbai, Bengaluru feeds |
| `GLOBAL_COUNTRIES` | Country feeds | Guardian/France24 country feeds |
| `GLOBAL_CITIES` | Global cities | London, NYC, Singapore |

### Source Inventory

- **Total Sources:** 223
- **Countries Covered:** 45
- **Cities Covered:** 23
- **Topic Domains:** 23 (geopolitics, finance, tech, science, etc.)

---

## 🔧 Configuration

### User Config Directory

IGS stores configuration in:

- **Linux/macOS:** `~/.config/igs-mcp/`
- **Windows:** `%APPDATA%\igs-mcp\`
- **Custom:** Set `IGS_CONFIG_DIR` environment variable

### Files

```
~/.config/igs-mcp/
├── pools.yml      # Pool definitions
├── sources.yml    # News sources (223 configured)
└── settings.yml   # HTTP/cache settings
```

### Add Custom Source

Edit `~/.config/igs-mcp/sources.yml`:

```yaml
- id: my_tech_blog
  name: My Tech Blog
  type: rss
  url: https://example.com/feed.xml
  parser: rss
  pools: [GLOBAL_TECH_CYBER]
  countries: [US]
  domains: [tech]
  is_active: true
```

No restart required — changes load automatically.

---

## 🏗️ Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│           MCP Client (Claude)               │
└─────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                  │ stdio
┌─────────────────▼───────────────────────────┐
│         IGS MCP Server (Node.js)            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Tools: 11 MCP tools                        │
│  • news.fetch      • research.search        │
│  • reddit.search   • research.paper         │
│  • sources.*       • pools.*                │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Parsers: 9 parsers                         │
│  • RSS/Atom        • Generic HTML           │
│  • Semantic Scholar• PDF extraction         │
│  • OFAC, WHO, USSF (custom HTML)            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  APIs:                                      │
│  • Reddit Search   • arXiv OAI-PMH          │
│  • Semantic Scholar• Google News proxy      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## ⚡ Performance

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Cold start | ~2 seconds |
| news.fetch (9 sources) | ~3 seconds |
| news.fetch (223 sources) | ~25 seconds |
| Cache hit ratio | ~90% (warm) |
| Memory usage | 50-150 MB |

---

## 🔒 Security

- ✅ **Local-only** — Runs on your machine
- ✅ **No telemetry** — No data collection
- ✅ **HTTPS only** — All external requests encrypted
- ✅ **No API keys required** — For basic functionality
- ✅ **User-controlled config** — You own your data

---

## 📦 Dependencies

### Runtime (15)

- @modelcontextprotocol/sdk — MCP protocol
- cheerio — HTML parsing
- pdf-parse — PDF text extraction
- xml2js — arXiv XML parsing
- p-queue — Concurrency control
- undici — HTTP client
- zod — Schema validation
- js-yaml — YAML parsing
- rss-parser — RSS/Atom parsing
- pino — Logging
- compromise — NLP (enrichment)
- sentiment — Sentiment analysis
- keyword-extractor — Keyword extraction
- date-fns + date-fns-tz — Date handling

### Development (5)

- typescript
- @types/node
- @types/js-yaml
- @types/xml2js
- ts-node-dev

---

## 🧪 Development

```bash
# Install
npm install

# Development mode (auto-reload)
npm run dev

# Build
npm run build

# Type check
npm run typecheck

# Start production
npm start
```

---

## 🐛 Troubleshooting

### Server won't start

```bash
# Check Node version (must be v20+)
node --version

# Rebuild
rm -rf dist/
npm run build
```

### Config not loading

```bash
# Check user config directory
ls -la ~/.config/igs-mcp/

# Force bootstrap
rm -rf ~/.config/igs-mcp/
npm start
```

### Cache issues

```bash
# Clear all caches
rm -rf ~/.config/igs-mcp/cache/
rm -rf ./cache/
```

### Rate limiting

- **Semantic Scholar:** 100 requests/day (free tier)
- **Reddit:** ~60 requests/minute
- **arXiv:** 3000 requests/day

Tool includes automatic retry with backoff.

---

## 📝 Changelog

### v0.2.0 (April 2026)

**Major Upgrade: Research Tools**

- ✨ NEW: `reddit.search` — Dynamic Reddit search
- ✨ NEW: `research.search` — arXiv + Semantic Scholar
- ✨ NEW: `research.paper` — PDF extraction with citations
- 🔧 Parallel source fetching (6 concurrent)
- 🔧 Historical date queries via Google News URL rewriting
- 🗑️ REMOVED: RSS-based Reddit/arXiv/Semantic Scholar sources
- 📚 Added INSTALL.md and AUDIT_REPORT.md
- 🛠️ Added `setup.sh` one-line installer with auto-config

### v0.1.0 (Initial Release)

- Core news fetching with pools/sources
- RSS/HTTP parsers
- YAML configuration
- Local caching with ETag support

10:   link?: string;
11:   date?: string;
12:   desc?: string;
13: };
14: 
15: export async function parseWithSelectors(source: Source, html: string, selectors: SelectorConfig): Promise<NewsItem[]> {
16:   const $ = loadHtml(html);
17:   const out: NewsItem[] = [];
18:   $(selectors.item).each((_i, el) => {
19:     try {
20:       const scope = $(el);
21:       let link = selectors.link ? scope.find(selectors.link).attr('href') : scope.find('a').attr('href');
22:       if (!link) return;
23:       const abs = new URL(link, source.url).toString();
24:       const titleText = selectors.title ? scope.find(selectors.title).text() : scope.find('a, h1, h2, h3').first().text();
25:       const title = stripHtml(titleText).slice(0, 500) || 'Untitled';
26:       let pubDate = new Date().toISOString();
27:       if (selectors.date) {
28:         const dateVal = scope.find(selectors.date).attr('datetime') || scope.find(selectors.date).text();
29:         if (dateVal) {
30:           const d = new Date(dateVal);
31:           if (!isNaN(d.getTime())) pubDate = d.toISOString();
32:         }
33:       } else {
34:         const timeEl = scope.find('time');
35:         const dt = timeEl.attr('datetime') || timeEl.text();
36:         if (dt) {
37:           const d = new Date(dt);
38:           if (!isNaN(d.getTime())) pubDate = d.toISOString();
39:         }
40:       }
41:       const descRaw = selectors.desc ? scope.find(selectors.desc).text() : scope.find('p').first().text();
42:       const content_snippet = stripHtml(descRaw).slice(0, 600);
43:       out.push({
44:         id: abs,
45:         title,
46:         link: abs,
47:         pubDate,
48:         source_name: source.name,
49:         pool_id: source.pools[0] || 'UNKNOWN',
50:         content_snippet,
51:       });
52:     } catch {}
53:   });
54:   return out;
55: }
56: 
57: // Heuristic auto parser for HTML pages without RSS: try common item containers and scoring
58: const ITEM_CANDIDATE_SELECTORS = [
59:   'article',
60:   'div[class*=article]',
61:   'div[class*=post]',
62:   'div[class*=card]',
63:   'div[class*=listing]',
64:   'div[class*=story]',
65:   'div[class*=headline]',
66:   'div[class*=teaser]',
67:   'div.views-row',
68:   'div[class*=node]',
69:   'li[class*=article]',
70:   'li[class*=post]',
71:   'section[class*=list] article',
72:   'section[class*=list] li',
73:   'a.card',
74:   'a[href*="/news/"]',
75: ];
76: 
77: function scoreItem($: any, el: any): number {
78:   const scope = $(el as any);
79:   const a = scope.find('a').first();
80:   const href = a.attr('href');
81:   const text = stripHtml(a.text() || '') || stripHtml(scope.text() || '');
82:   let score = 0;
83:   if (href) score += 2;
84:   if (text.length > 20) score += 2;
85:   const timeEl = scope.find('time');
86:   if (timeEl.length) score += 1;
87:   if (/\/(20\d{2}|19\d{2})\//.test(href || '')) score += 1;
88:   return score;
89: }
90: 
91: export async function autoParseHtml(source: Source, html: string): Promise<{ items: NewsItem[]; usedSelector?: string }> {
92:   const $ = loadHtml(html);
93:   let best: { selector: string; items: NewsItem[]; score: number } | null = null;
94:   for (const sel of ITEM_CANDIDATE_SELECTORS) {
95:     const nodes = $(sel);
96:     if (!nodes.length) continue;
97:     const items: NewsItem[] = [];
98:     nodes.each((_i, el: any) => {
99:       const s = scoreItem($ as any, el as any);
100:       if (s < 3) return;
101:       try {
102:         const scope = $(el);
103:         const a = (el?.name === 'a') ? scope : scope.find('a').first();
104:         let href = a.attr('href');
105:         if (!href) return;
106:         const abs = new URL(href, source.url).toString();
107:         const titleText = a.text() || scope.find('h1,h2,h3').first().text();
108:         const title = stripHtml(titleText).slice(0, 500) || 'Untitled';
109:         let pubDate = new Date().toISOString();
110:         const timeEl = scope.find('time');
111:         const dt = timeEl.attr('datetime') || timeEl.text();
112:         if (dt) {
113:           const d = new Date(dt);
114:           if (!isNaN(d.getTime())) pubDate = d.toISOString();
115:         }
116:         const para = scope.find('p').first().text();
117:         const content_snippet = stripHtml(para).slice(0, 600);
118:         items.push({ id: abs, title, link: abs, pubDate, source_name: source.name, pool_id: source.pools[0] || 'UNKNOWN', content_snippet });
119:       } catch {}
120:     });
121:     const good = items.length;
122:     const avgTitleLen = items.reduce((a, b) => a + b.title.length, 0) / (good || 1);
123:     const score = good * 2 + (avgTitleLen > 30 ? 1 : 0);
124:     if (!best || score > best.score) best = { selector: sel, items, score };
125:   }
126:   return { items: best?.items || [], usedSelector: best?.selector };
127: }
128: 
129: // Explore common site routes to find a good list page, then parse
130: const COMMON_PATHS = ['/', '/news', '/latest', '/world', '/politics', '/business', '/technology', '/tech', '/opinion', '/opinions', '/culture', '/india', '/international', '/world-news', '/latest-news', '/economy', '/analysis'];
131: 
132: export async function autodiscoverList(source: Source, http: HttpClient): Promise<{ kind: 'rss'|'atom'|'html'|'json'|'sitemap'|'none'; url?: string; selectors?: SelectorConfig; sample?: NewsItem[] }> {
133:   // Fetch base page first
134:   const pathsToTry = new Set(COMMON_PATHS);
135:   // add top nav links from homepage
136:   try {
137:     const homeRes = await http.fetch(new URL('/', source.url).toString(), {}, 'bypass');
138:     if ('response' in homeRes) {
139:       const $ = loadHtml(homeRes.response.bodyText);
140:       $('nav a[href^="/"]').slice(0, 10).each((_i, el) => {
141:         const href = $(el).attr('href');
142:         if (href) pathsToTry.add(href);
143:       });
144:     }
145:   } catch {}
146: 
147:   for (const p of Array.from(pathsToTry).slice(0, 20)) {
148:     try {
149:       const url = new URL(p, source.url).toString();
150:       const res = await http.fetch(url, {}, 'bypass');
151:       if (!('response' in res)) continue;
152:       const ctype = (res.response.headers['content-type'] || '').toLowerCase();
153:       const body = res.response.bodyText;
154:       if (ctype.includes('xml') || /<rss[\s>/]/i.test(body) || /<feed[\s>/]/i.test(body)) {
155:         return { kind: 'rss', url, sample: [] };
156:       }
157:       if (ctype.includes('json') || /^[\[{]/.test(body.trim())) {
158:         return { kind: 'json', url };
159:       }
160:       // Try rel=alternate for RSS on this page
161:       const $ = loadHtml(body);
162:       const linkEl = $("link[rel='alternate'][type*='rss'], link[rel='alternate'][type*='atom']").first();
163:       const href = linkEl.attr('href');
164:       if (href) {
165:         const feedUrl = new URL(href, url).toString();
166:         return { kind: 'rss', url: feedUrl };
167:       }
168:       // Otherwise attempt autoParseHtml
169:       const { items, usedSelector } = await autoParseHtml(source, body);
170:       if ((items?.length || 0) >= 5 && usedSelector) {
171:         return { kind: 'html', url, selectors: { item: usedSelector }, sample: items.slice(0, 10) };
172:       }
173:     } catch {}
174:   }
175:   // Sitemaps as a last resort
176:   try {
177:     const items = await sampleFromSitemap(source, http);
178:     if (items && items.length >= 10) {
179:       return { kind: 'sitemap', url: new URL('/sitemap.xml', source.url).toString(), sample: items.slice(0, 10) };
180:     }
181:   } catch {}
182:   return { kind: 'none' };
183: }

1: import { load as loadXml } from 'cheerio';
2: import type { NewsItem, Source } from '../types/news.js';
3: import type { HttpClient } from '../http/client.js';
4: import { stripHtml } from '../normalize/html.js';
5: 
6: async function tryFetch(http: HttpClient, url: string) {
7:   try { const res = await http.fetch(url, {}, 'bypass'); return ('response' in res) ? res.response.bodyText : null; } catch { return null; }
8: }
9: 
10: export async function findSitemaps(baseUrl: string, http: HttpClient): Promise<string[]> {
11:   const urls: string[] = [];
12:   const robots = await tryFetch(http, new URL('/robots.txt', baseUrl).toString());
13:   if (robots) {
14:     for (const line of robots.split(/\r?\n/)) {
15:       const m = line.match(/Sitemap:\s*(\S+)/i);
16:       if (m) urls.push(m[1]);
17:     }
18:   }
19:   if (urls.length === 0) {
20:     urls.push(new URL('/sitemap.xml', baseUrl).toString());
21:     urls.push(new URL('/sitemap_index.xml', baseUrl).toString());
22:   }
23:   return Array.from(new Set(urls));
24: }
25: 
26: export function parseSitemapXml(xml: string): { loc: string; lastmod?: string }[] {
27:   const $ = loadXml(xml, { xmlMode: true });
28:   const out: { loc: string; lastmod?: string }[] = [];
29:   // sitemapindex -> sitemap
30:   $('sitemapindex sitemap loc').each((_i, el) => {
31:     const loc = $(el).text().trim();
32:     if (loc) out.push({ loc });
33:   });
34:   if (out.length) return out;
35:   // urlset -> url
36:   const out2: { loc: string; lastmod?: string }[] = [];
37:   $('urlset url').each((_i, el) => {
38:     const loc = $(el).find('loc').text().trim();
39:     if (!loc) return;
40:     const lastmod = $(el).find('lastmod').text().trim();
41:     out2.push({ loc, lastmod });
42:   });
43:   return out2;
44: }
45: 
46: export async function sampleFromSitemap(source: Source, http: HttpClient): Promise<NewsItem[] | null> {
47:   const candidates = await findSitemaps(source.url, http);
48:   for (const smUrl of candidates.slice(0, 3)) {
49:     const xml = await tryFetch(http, smUrl);
50:     if (!xml) continue;
51:     const entries = parseSitemapXml(xml);
52:     if (!entries.length) continue;
53:     // If this is a sitemap index, try to pick likely news sitemap
54:     if (entries[0].loc && !entries[0].lastmod && entries.length && entries[0].loc.endsWith('.xml')) {
55:       const filtered = entries.filter(e => /news|latest|world|article|stories/i.test(e.loc));
56:       const pick = (filtered[0] || entries[0]).loc;
57:       const xml2 = await tryFetch(http, pick);
58:       if (!xml2) continue;
59:       const urls = parseSitemapXml(xml2).slice(0, 50);
60:       if (!urls.length) continue;
61:       return urls.map(u => toItem(source, u.loc, u.lastmod));
62:     }
63:     // Already a urlset
64:     const urls = entries.slice(0, 50);
65:     if (urls.length) return urls.map(u => toItem(source, u.loc, u.lastmod));
66:   }
67:   return null;
68: }
69: 
70: function toItem(source: Source, loc: string, lastmod?: string): NewsItem {
71:   const title = stripHtml(decodeURIComponent(loc.split('/').pop() || '')).replace(/[-_]/g, ' ').slice(0, 120) || 'Untitled';
72:   let pubDate = new Date().toISOString();
73:   if (lastmod) {
74:     const d = new Date(lastmod);
75:     if (!isNaN(d.getTime())) pubDate = d.toISOString();
76:   }
77:   return {
78:     id: loc,
79:     title,
80:     link: loc,
81:     pubDate,
82:     source_name: source.name,
83:     pool_id: source.pools[0] || 'UNKNOWN',
84:     content_snippet: '',
85:   };
86: }

1: /**
2:  * test_all_sources.ts — Comprehensive test of ALL active news sources.
3:  *
4:  * Iterates every active source, fetches its URL, parses the response,
5:  * and reports pass/fail per pool. Designed for bulk verification.
6:  *
7:  * Usage: npx tsx scripts/test_all_sources.ts
8:  */
9: import path from 'node:path';
10: import { loadSettings, loadSources, getUserConfigDir } from '../src/config/loader.js';
11: import { HttpClient } from '../src/http/client.js';
12: import { parseRss } from '../src/parsers/rss.js';

14: import { parseUssfcfcHtml } from '../src/parsers/ussf_cfc.js';

21: import type { Source, NewsItem } from '../src/types/news.js';

23: // ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
24: 
25: async function parseBySource(source: Source, http: HttpClient): Promise<{ items: NewsItem[]; succeeded: boolean; error?: string }> {
26:   try {
27:     const headers = source.headers || {};
28:     const res = await http.fetch(source.url, headers, 'bypass');
29: 
30:     if (!('response' in res)) {
31:       return { items: [], succeeded: false, error: 'No response (304 or network error)' };
32:     }
33: 
34:     const body = res.response.bodyText;
35:     const ctype = (res.response.headers['content-type'] || '').toLowerCase();
36:     let items: NewsItem[] = [];
37: 
38:     if (source.parser === 'ofac') {
39:       items = parseOfacHtml(source, body);
40:     } else if (source.parser === 'ussf_cfc') {
41:       items = parseUssfcfcHtml(source, body);
42:     } else if (source.parser === 'who_dons') {
43:       items = parseWhoJson(source, body);
44:     } else if (source.parser === 'newslaundry') {
45:       items = parseNewslaundryHtml(source, body);
46:     } else if (source.parser === 'semantic_scholar') {
47:       items = parseSemanticScholarJson(source, body);
48:     } else if (source.parser === 'generic_html' && source.parserConfig?.selectors) {
49:       items = await parseWithSelectors(source, body, source.parserConfig.selectors as any);
50:     } else {
51:       const looksXml = ctype.includes('xml') || ctype.includes('rss') || ctype.includes('atom')
52:         || /<rss[\s>/]/i.test(body) || /<feed[\s>/]/i.test(body);
53:       if (looksXml) {
54:         items = await parseRss(source, body, true);
55:       } else if (ctype.includes('json') || /^[\[{]/.test(body.trim())) {
56:         items = parseJsonFeed(source, body);
57:       } else {
58:         try {
59:           const $ = loadHtml(body);
60:           const linkEl = $("link[rel='alternate'][type*='rss'], link[rel='alternate'][type*='atom']").first();
61:           const href = linkEl.attr('href');
62:           if (href) {
63:             const rssUrl = new URL(href, source.url).toString();
64:             const res2 = await http.fetch(rssUrl, headers, 'bypass');
65:             if ('response' in res2) {
66:               items = await parseRss(source, res2.response.bodyText, true);
67:             }
68:           } else {
69:             const auto = await autoParseHtml(source, body);
70:             items = auto.items;
71:           }
72:         } catch { items = []; }
73:       }
74:     }
75: 
76:     const succeeded = items.length > 0;
77:     return { items, succeeded, error: succeeded ? undefined : '0 items returned (empty feed/parse)' };
78:   } catch (e: any) {
79:     return { items: [], succeeded: false, error: e?.message?.slice(0, 120) || String(e) };
80:   }
81: }
82: 
83: // ── Main ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
84: 
85: interface PoolStats {
86:   pool: string;
87:   total: number;
88:   passed: number;
89:   failed: number;
90:   failures: { id: string; name: string; error: string }[];

93: async function main() {
94:   console.log('═'.repeat(72));
95:   console.log('  IGS — Full Source Health Audit');
96:   console.log(`  Started: ${new Date().toISOString()}`);

107:

109:   console.log(`\nLoaded ${sf.sources.length} sources total, ${sources.length} active.\n`);
110: 
111:   // Per-pool tracking
112:   const poolMap = new Map<string, PoolStats>();
113:   function getPoolStats(pool: string): PoolStats {
114:     if (!poolMap.has(pool)) {
115:       poolMap.set(pool, { pool, total: 0, passed: 0, failed: 0, failures: [] });
116:     }
117:     return poolMap.get(pool)!;

120:   // Track grand total

123:   const allFailures: { id: string; name: string; pool: string; error: string }[] = [];
124:   const sourceResults: { id: string; name: string; pool: string; ok: boolean; items: number; error?: string }[] = [];
125: 
126:   // Process each source sequentially to avoid overwhelming hosts

134: 
135:     const stats = getPoolStats(primaryPool);
136:     stats.total++;

139:       totalPassed++;
140:       stats.passed++;
141:       process.stdout.write(`✅ ${items.length} items\n`);
142:     } else {
143:       totalFailed++;
144:       stats.failed++;
145:       stats.failures.push({ id: src.id, name: src.name, error: error || 'Unknown' });
146:       allFailures.push({ id: src.id, name: src.name, pool: primaryPool, error: error || 'Unknown' });
147:       process.stdout.write(`❌ ${error || 'FAILED'}\n`);
148:     }
149: 
150:     sourceResults.push({ id: src.id, name: src.name, pool: primaryPool, ok: succeeded, items: items.length, error });
151:   }
152: 
153:   // ── Report ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
154:   console.log('\n' + '═'.repeat(72));
155:   console.log('  RESULTS');
156:   console.log('═'.repeat(72));
157:   console.log(`  Total: ${sources.length} sources`);
158:   console.log(`  Passed: ${totalPassed}`);
159:   console.log(`  Failed: ${totalFailed}`);
160:   console.log(`  Pass rate: ${(totalPassed / sources.length * 100).toFixed(1)}%`);
161:

164:   console.log('  PER-POOL BREAKDOWN');

167:   for (const [, stats] of sortedPools) {

169:     const status = stats.failed === 0 ? '✅' : '⚠️';
170:     console.log(`  ${status} ${stats.pool.padEnd(25)} ${stats.passed.toString().padStart(3)}/${stats.total.toString().padStart(3)}  (${rate}%)`);
171:   }

174:   if (allFailures.length > 0) {
175:     console.log('\n' + '─'.repeat(72));
176:     console.log(`  FAILED SOURCES (${allFailures.length})`);
177:     console.log('─'.repeat(72));
178: 
179:     // Group by pool for readability
180:     const failuresByPool = new Map<string, typeof allFailures>();
181:     for (const f of allFailures) {
182:       if (!failuresByPool.has(f.pool)) failuresByPool.set(f.pool, []);
183:       failuresByPool.get(f.pool)!.push(f);
184:     }
185: 
186:     for (const [pool, failures] of failuresByPool) {
187:       console.log(`\n  📁 ${pool} (${failures.length} failed):`);

191:     }

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # IGS Source Expansion — Learnings
2: 
3: ## Task: Add `platform`, `tier`, `rate_limit` to Source type/schema
4: 
5: ### Files Modified
6: - `src/types/news.ts` — Source type updated
7: - `src/config/schemas.ts` — SourceSchema updated
8: 
9: ### Changes Made
10: 
11: #### Source type changes:
12: - `type` expanded: `'rss' | 'http'` → `'rss' | 'http' | 'social_media'`
13: - `sourceCategory` expanded: `'news' | 'community' | 'research'` → `'news' | 'community' | 'research' | 'social'`
14: - New field: `platform?: 'reddit' | 'twitter' | 'web'`
15: - New field: `tier?: 1 | 2 | 3`
16: - New field: `rate_limit?: { interval_seconds: number; batch_size?: number }`
17: 
18: #### SourceSchema changes:
19: - `type` enum expanded similarly with z.enum
20: - `platform`: `z.enum(['reddit', 'twitter', 'web']).optional()`
21: - `tier`: `z.union([z.literal(1), z.literal(2), z.literal(3)]).optional()`
22: - `rate_limit`: `z.object({ interval_seconds: z.number().int().positive(), batch_size: z.number().int().positive().optional() }).optional()`
23: - `sourceCategory` enum expanded + kept `optional().default('news')`
24: 
25: ### Key Decisions
26: - All new fields are OPTIONAL — backward compatible with existing sources.yml
27: - `platform` defaults to undefined (backward compat implied as 'web' at usage time)
28: - `tier` defaults to undefined (implied Tier 2 at usage time)
29: - `rate_limit` entirely optional — no rate limiting unless explicitly configured
30: - Placed new fields between `is_active` and `sourceCategory` in both type and schema
31: 
32: ### Build Result
33: - `bun run build` passes with zero errors

(End of file - total 33 lines)
</content>

# IGS Source Expansion — Learnings

## Task: Add `platform`, `tier`, `rate_limit` to Source type/schema

### Files Modified
- `src/types/news.ts` — Source type updated
- `src/config/schemas.ts` — SourceSchema updated

### Changes Made

#### Source type changes:
- `type` expanded: `'rss' | 'http'` → `'rss' | 'http' | 'social_media'`
- `sourceCategory` expanded: `'news' | 'community' | 'research'` → `'news' | 'community' | 'research' | 'social'`
- New field: `platform?: 'reddit' | 'twitter' | 'web'`
- New field: `tier?: 1 | 2 | 3`
- New field: `rate_limit?: { interval_seconds: number; batch_size?: number }`

#### SourceSchema changes:
- `type` enum expanded similarly with z.enum
- `platform`: `z.enum(['reddit', 'twitter', 'web']).optional()`

state.output:
<path>/home/ishanp/Documents/GitHub/igs-mcp/scripts/validate-sources.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env bun
2: /**
3:  * validate-sources.ts — Pre-validation script for RSS/JSON feeds and other sources.
4:  *
5:  * Tests each source URL for:
6:  *   1. HTTP 200 status
7:  *   2. Content-Type header inspection
8:  *   3. Parsing simulation (where possible)
9:  *
10:  * Usage:
11:  *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser>
12:  *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser> [--url <url2> --parser <parser2> ...]
13:  *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --file <path-to-yaml>
14:  *
15:  * YAML file format (matching sources.yml):
16:  *   sources:
17:  *     - url: https://example.com/feed
18:  *       parser: rss
19:  *       name: optional label
20:  *
21:  * Or a flat list:
22:  *   - url: https://example.com/feed
23:  *     parser: rss
24:  *
25:  * Exit code: 0 if all pass, 1 if any fail
26:  */
27: 
28: import RSSParser from 'rss-parser';
29: import { request } from 'undici';
30: import yaml from 'js-yaml';
31: import fs from 'node:fs/promises';
32: 
33: const rssParser = new RSSParser({ timeout: 15000 });
34: 
35: // ── Types ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
36: 
37: interface SourceEntry {
38:   url: string;
39:   parser: string;
40:   name?: string;
41: }
42: 
43: interface ValidationResult {
44:   url: string;
45:   parser: string;
46:   status: 'ok' | 'fail' | 'partial';
47:   items_count: number;
48:   error?: string;
49:   content_type?: string;
50:   feed_type?: string;
51:   sample_title?: string;
52: }
53: 
54: interface Summary {
55:   total: number;
56:   passed: number;
57:   failed: number;
58:   partial: number;
59: }
60: 
61: // ── CLI argument parsing ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
62: 
63: function parseArgs(): { entries: SourceEntry[]; filePath: string | null } {
64:   const args = process.argv.slice(2);
65:   const entries: SourceEntry[] = [];
66:   let filePath: string | null = null;
67: 
68:   const fileIndex = args.indexOf('--file');
69:   if (fileIndex !== -1 && fileIndex + 1 < args.length) {
70:     filePath = args[fileIndex + 1];
71:   }
72: 
73:   for (let i = 0; i < args.length; i++) {
74:     if (args[i] === '--url' && i + 1 < args.length) {
75:       const url = args[i + 1];
76:       if (!url.startsWith('http://') && !url.startsWith('https://')) {
77:         console.error(`Invalid URL: ${url}`);
78:         process.exit(1);
79:       }
80:       // Look ahead for the associated --parser
81:       let parser = 'rss';
82:       for (let j = i + 2; j < args.length; j++) {
83:         if (args[j] === '--url' || args[j] === '--file') break;
84:         if (args[j] === '--parser' && j + 1 < args.length) {
85:           parser = args[j + 1];
86:           break;
87:         }
88:       }
89:       entries.push({ url, parser });
90:     }
91:   }
92: 
93:   return { entries, filePath };
94: }
95: 
96: async function loadSourcesFromYaml(filePath: string): Promise<SourceEntry[]> {
97:   const raw = await fs.readFile(filePath, 'utf-8');
98:   const data = yaml.load(raw) as any;
99: 
100:   if (!data) throw new Error(`Empty or invalid YAML file: ${filePath}`);
101: 
102:   // Support both { sources: [...] } and flat [...] formats
103:   const list: any[] = data.sources ?? (Array.isArray(data) ? data : null);
104:   if (!list) throw new Error(`YAML file must have a "sources" key or be a flat list`);
105: 
106:   return list.map((entry: any, idx: number) => {
107:     if (!entry.url) throw new Error(`Entry ${idx} is missing "url"`);
108:     if (!entry.parser) throw new Error(`Entry ${idx} is missing "parser"`);
109:     return { url: entry.url, parser: entry.parser, name: entry.name };
110:   });
111: }
112: 
113: // ── Feed type detection helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────
114: 
115: function detectFeedType(body: string, contentType: string): string {
116:   const trimmed = body.trim();
117:   if (trimmed.startsWith('<?xml') || trimmed.startsWith('<rss') || trimmed.startsWith('<feed')) {
118:     if (/<rss[\s>/]/i.test(trimmed)) return 'rss';
119:     if (/<feed[\s>/]/i.test(trimmed)) return 'atom';
120:     if (contentType.includes('xml')) return 'xml';
121:     return 'xml';
122:   }
123:   if (trimmed.startsWith('{') || trimmed.startsWith('[')) {
124:     try {
125:       const parsed = JSON.parse(trimmed);
126:       if (parsed?.version && parsed?.items) return 'json_feed';
127:       if (parsed?.items) return 'json_feed_like';
128:       return 'json';
129:     } catch {
130:       return 'json_like';
131:     }
132:   }
133:   if (trimmed.startsWith('%PDF')) return 'pdf';
134:   return 'html';
135: }
136: 
137: function getContentTypeCategory(ctype: string): string {
138:   const ct = ctype.toLowerCase();
139:   if (ct.includes('rss')) return 'application/rss+xml';
140:   if (ct.includes('atom')) return 'application/atom+xml';
141:   if (ct.includes('json')) return 'application/json';
142:   if (ct.includes('xml')) return 'application/xml';
143:   if (ct.includes('html')) return 'text/html';
144:   if (ct.includes('pdf')) return 'application/pdf';
145:   return ct.split(';')[0] || 'unknown';
146: }
147: 
148: // ── Validation per parser type ───────────────────────────────────────────────
149: 
150: async function validateSource(entry: SourceEntry): Promise<ValidationResult> {
151:   const { url, parser } = entry;
152:   const result: ValidationResult = {
153:     url,
154:     parser,
155:     status: 'fail',
156:     items_count: 0,
157:   };
158: 
159:   // 1. HTTP fetch
160:   let body: string;
161:   let statusCode: number;
162:   let rawContentType: string;
163: 
164:   try {
165:     const controller = new AbortController();
166:     const timeout = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), 15000);
167: 
168:     const resp = await request(url, {
169:       method: 'GET',
170:       headers: {
171:         'User-Agent': 'IGS-Validator/1.0 (+https://github.com/ishan-parihar/igs-mcp)',
172:         Accept: 'application/rss+xml, application/json, text/html, application/xml, application/pdf, */*',
173:       },
174:       signal: controller.signal,
175:     });
176: 
177:     clearTimeout(timeout);
178: 
179:     statusCode = resp.statusCode;
180:     rawContentType = (resp.headers['content-type'] as string) || '';
181:     result.content_type = rawContentType;
182: 
183:     // Check HTTP status
184:     if (statusCode !== 200) {
185:       result.status = 'fail';
186:       result.error = `HTTP ${statusCode}${resp.statusMessage ? ` ${resp.statusMessage}` : ''}`;
187:       return result;
188:     }
189: 
190:     // Read body
191:     const chunks: Buffer[] = [];
192:     for await (const chunk of resp.body) {
193:       chunks.push(Buffer.isBuffer(chunk) ? chunk : Buffer.from(chunk));
194:     }
195:     body = Buffer.concat(chunks).toString('utf-8');
196: 
197:     if (body.length === 0) {
198:       result.status = 'fail';
199:       result.error = 'Empty response body';
200:       return result;
201:     }
202:   } catch (err: any) {
203:     result.status = 'fail';
204:     if (err.name === 'AbortError') {
205:       result.error = 'timeout (15s)';
206:     } else if (err.code === 'ENOTFOUND') {
207:       result.error = `DNS resolution failed: ${err.message}`;
208:     } else if (err.code === 'ECONNREFUSED') {
209:       result.error = `Connection refused: ${err.message}`;
210:     } else if (err.code === 'ECONNRESET') {
211:       result.error = `Connection reset: ${err.message}`;
212:     } else if (err.code === 'UND_ERR_HEADERS_TIMEOUT') {
213:       result.error = 'Headers timeout';
214:     } else if (err.type === 'aborted' || err.message?.includes('abort')) {
215:       result.error = 'timeout (15s)';
216:     } else {
217:       result.error = err.message?.slice(0, 200) || String(err);
218:     }
219:     return result;
220:   }
221: 
222:   // 2. Detect feed type from content
223:   result.feed_type = detectFeedType(body, rawContentType);
224: 
225:   // 3. Simulate parsing
226:   try {
227:     await simulateParse(parser, body, result, url);
228:   } catch (parseErr: any) {
229:     result.status = 'fail';
230:     result.error = `Parse error: ${parseErr.message?.slice(0, 200) || String(parseErr)}`;
231:   }
232: 
233:   return result;
234: }
235: 
236: async function simulateParse(
237:   parser: string,
238:   body: string,
239:   result: ValidationResult,
240:   sourceUrl: string,
241: ): Promise<void> {
242:   switch (parser) {
243:     case 'rss': {
244:       let feed;
245:       try {
246:         feed = await rssParser.parseString(body);
247:       } catch {
248:         // rss-parser may also handle Atom; if it fails, try as raw XML
249:         feed = { items: [] };
250:       }
251:       const items = feed.items || [];
252:       result.items_count = items.length;
253: 
254:       if (items.length > 0) {
255:         result.status = 'ok';
256:         result.sample_title = items[0]?.title?.slice(0, 120);
257:       } else {
258:         // Check if body at least looks like valid RSS/XML
259:         if (body.includes('<item>') || body.includes('<entry>') || body.includes('<rss') || body.includes('<feed')) {
260:           result.status = 'partial';
261:           result.error = 'Feed parsed but 0 items';
262:         } else {
263:           result.status = 'fail';
264:           result.error = 'Content does not appear to be RSS/Atom XML';
265:         }
266:       }
267:       break;
268:     }
269: 
270:     case 'json_feed': {
271:       let data: any;
272:       try {
273:         data = JSON.parse(body);
274:       } catch {
275:         result.status = 'fail';
276:         result.error = 'Invalid JSON';
277:         return;
278:       }
279: 
280:       const items = Array.isArray(data?.items) ? data.items
281:         : Array.isArray(data) ? data
282:         : Array.isArray(data?.value) ? data.value
283:         : [];
284: 
285:       result.items_count = items.length;
286: 
287:       const hasVersion = typeof data?.version === 'string' || data?.version !== undefined;
288:       const hasTitle = typeof data?.title === 'string';
289: 
290:       if (items.length > 0) {
291:         result.status = 'ok';
292:         result.sample_title = items[0]?.title || items[0]?.url || '(no title)';
293:         result.sample_title = String(result.sample_title).slice(0, 120);
294:       } else if (hasVersion || hasTitle) {
295:         result.status = 'partial';
296:         result.error = 'JSON feed structure recognized but 0 items';
297:       } else if (Array.isArray(data) && data.length > 0) {
298:         // Could be an array of items (some APIs return this)
299:         result.status = 'ok';
300:         result.items_count = data.length;
301:         result.sample_title = String(data[0]?.title || data[0]?.url || '').slice(0, 120);
302:       } else {
303:         result.status = 'fail';
304:         result.error = 'JSON does not match feed structure (missing version/items)';
305:       }
306:       break;
307:     }
308: 
309:     case 'generic_html':
310:     case 'article_content': {
311:       // Check for non-empty HTML body
312:       const stripped = body.replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '').trim();
313:       if (stripped.length > 0) {
314:         result.status = 'partial';
315:         result.items_count = 0;
316:         result.feed_type = result.feed_type || 'html';
317:       } else {
318:         result.status = 'fail';
319:         result.error = 'Empty or whitespace-only HTML body';
320:       }
321:       break;
322:     }
323: 
324:     case 'sitemap': {
325:       // Check for sitemap XML structure
326:       const trimmed = body.trim();
327:       if (trimmed.includes('<urlset') || trimmed.includes('<sitemapindex')) {
328:         // Count urls
329:         const locMatches = body.match(/<loc>/g);
330:         result.items_count = locMatches ? locMatches.length : 0;
331:         result.status = result.items_count > 0 ? 'ok' : 'partial';
332:         if (result.items_count === 0) result.error = 'Sitemap parsed but 0 URLs found';
333:       } else if (trimmed.startsWith('<?xml') || trimmed.startsWith('<')) {
334:         result.status = 'partial';
335:         result.items_count = 0;
336:         result.error = 'XML content but not a recognized sitemap format';
337:       } else {
338:         result.status = 'fail';
339:         result.error = 'Not a sitemap XML';
340:       }
341:       break;
342:     }
343: 
344:     case 'ofac':
345:     case 'ussf_cfc':
346:     case 'newslaundry': {
347:       // Custom HTML parsers: check for non-empty body
348:       const stripped = body.replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '').trim();
349:       if (stripped.length > 100) {
350:         result.status = 'partial';
351:         result.items_count = 0;
352:       } else {
353:         result.status = 'fail';
354:         result.error = `Body too short for ${parser} parser (${stripped.length} chars text)`;
355:       }
356:       break;
357:     }
358: 
359:     case 'who_dons':
360:     case 'semantic_scholar': {
361:       // Custom JSON parsers: verify parseable JSON
362:       try {
363:         const data = JSON.parse(body);
364:         result.status = 'partial';
365:         result.items_count = Array.isArray(data) ? data.length
366:           : Array.isArray(data?.items) ? data.items.length
367:           : Array.isArray(data?.results) ? data.results.length
368:           : 0;
369:         if (result.items_count === 0) result.error = 'JSON parsed but 0 items found in expected fields';
370:       } catch {
371:         result.status = 'fail';
372:         result.error = 'Invalid JSON for parser type';
373:       }
374:       break;
375:     }
376: 
377:     case 'pdf-extractor':
378:     case 'pdf-to-markdown': {
379:       // Check for PDF magic bytes
380:       if (body.startsWith('%PDF') || body.charCodeAt(0) === 0x25) {
381:         result.status = 'partial';
382:         result.items_count = 0;
383:         result.feed_type = 'pdf';
384:       } else {
385:         result.status = 'fail';
386:         result.error = 'Not a PDF file (missing %PDF header)';
387:       }
388:       break;
389:     }
390: 
391:     default: {
392:       // Unknown parser: just check HTTP 200
393:       result.status = 'partial';
394:       result.items_count = 0;
395:       result.error = `Unknown parser type "${parser}" — only connectivity verified`;
396:       break;
397:     }
398:   }
399: }
400: 
401: // ── Report ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
402: 
403: function printReport(results: ValidationResult[]): void {
404:   const summary: Summary = { total: 0, passed: 0, failed: 0, partial: 0 };
405: 
406:   for (const r of results) {
407:     summary.total++;
408:     if (r.status === 'ok') summary.passed++;
409:     else if (r.status === 'fail') summary.failed++;
410:     else if (r.status === 'partial') summary.partial++;
411:   }
412: 
413:   const report: Report = { summary, results };
414:   console.log(JSON.stringify(report, null, 2));
415: }
416: 
417: // ── Main ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
418: 
419: async function main() {
420:   const { entries: cliEntries, filePath } = parseArgs();
421:   let allEntries: SourceEntry[] = [...cliEntries];
422: 
423:   if (filePath) {
424:     try {
425:       const fileEntries = await loadSourcesFromYaml(filePath);
426:       allEntries = allEntries.concat(fileEntries);
427:     } catch (err: any) {
428:       console.error(`Error loading YAML file "${filePath}": ${err.message}`);
429:       process.exit(1);
430:     }
431:   }
432: 
433:   if (allEntries.length === 0) {
434:     console.error('No sources to validate. Provide --url/--parser pairs or --file.');
435:     console.error('');
436:     console.error('Usage:');
437:     console.error('  bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser>');
438:     console.error('  bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --file <path-to-yaml>');
439:     process.exit(1);
440:   }
441: 
442:   const results: ValidationResult[] = [];
443: 
444:   for (let i = 0; i < allEntries.length; i++) {
445:     const entry = allEntries[i];
446:     const label = entry.name || entry.url;
447:     process.stderr.write(`[${i + 1}/${allEntries.length}] ${label}... `);
448: 
449:     const result = await validateSource(entry);
450:     results.push(result);
451: 
452:     const icons: Record<string, string> = { ok: '✓', fail: '✗', partial: '~' };
453:     process.stderr.write(`${icons[result.status] || '?'} ${result.status}`);
454:     if (result.items_count > 0) process.stderr.write(` (${result.items_count} items)`);
455:     if (result.error) process.stderr.write(` — ${result.error.slice(0, 80)}`);
456:     process.stderr.write('\n');
457:   }
458: 
459:   printReport(results);
460: 
461:   const anyFailed = results.some(r => r.status === 'fail');
462:   process.exit(anyFailed ? 1 : 0);
463: }
464: 
465: main().catch(err => {
466:   console.error('Fatal error:', err);
467:   process.exit(1);
468: });

(End of file - total 468 lines)
</content>

#!/usr/bin/env bun
/**
 * validate-sources.ts — Pre-validation script for RSS/JSON feeds and other sources.
 *
 * Tests each source URL for:
 *   1. HTTP 200 status
 *   2. Content-Type header inspection
 *   3. Parsing simulation (where possible)
 *
 * Usage:
 *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser>
 *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser> [--url <url2> --parser <parser2> ...]
 *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --file <path-to-yaml>
 *
 * YAML file format (matching sources.yml):
 *   sources:
 *     - url: https://example.com/feed
 *       parser: rss
 *       name: optional label
 *

      let parser = 'rss';

<path>/home/ishanp/Documents/GitHub/igs-mcp/scripts/validate-sources.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env bun
2: /**
3:  * validate-sources.ts — Pre-validation script for RSS/JSON feeds and other sources.
4:  *
5:  * Tests each source URL for:
6:  *   1. HTTP 200 status
7:  *   2. Content-Type header inspection
8:  *   3. Parsing simulation (where possible)
9:  *
10:  * Usage:
11:  *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser>
12:  *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser> [--url <url2> --parser <parser2> ...]
13:  *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --file <path-to-yaml>
14:  *
15:  * YAML file format (matching sources.yml):
16:  *   sources:
17:  *     - url: https://example.com/feed
18:  *       parser: rss
19:  *       name: optional label
20:  *
21:  * Or a flat list:
22:  *   - url: https://example.com/feed
23:  *     parser: rss
24:  *
25:  * Exit code: 0 if all pass, 1 if any fail
26:  */
27: 
28: import RSSParser from 'rss-parser';
29: import { request } from 'undici';
30: import yaml from 'js-yaml';
31: import fs from 'node:fs/promises';
32: 
33: const rssParser = new RSSParser({ timeout: 15000 });
34: 
35: // ── Types ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
36: 
37: interface SourceEntry {
38:   url: string;
39:   parser: string;
40:   name?: string;
41: }
42: 
43: interface ValidationResult {
44:   url: string;
45:   parser: string;
46:   status: 'ok' | 'fail' | 'partial';
47:   items_count: number;
48:   error?: string;
49:   content_type?: string;
50:   feed_type?: string;
51:   sample_title?: string;
52: }
53: 
54: interface Summary {
55:   total: number;
56:   passed: number;
57:   failed: number;
58:   partial: number;
59: }
60: 
61: // ── CLI argument parsing ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
62: 
63: function parseArgs(): { entries: SourceEntry[]; filePath: string | null } {
64:   const args = process.argv.slice(2);
65:   const entries: SourceEntry[] = [];
66:   let filePath: string | null = null;
67: 
68:   const fileIndex = args.indexOf('--file');
69:   if (fileIndex !== -1 && fileIndex + 1 < args.length) {
70:     filePath = args[fileIndex + 1];
71:   }
72: 
73:   for (let i = 0; i < args.length; i++) {
74:     if (args[i] === '--url' && i + 1 < args.length) {
75:       const url = args[i + 1];
76:       if (!url.startsWith('http://') && !url.startsWith('https://')) {
77:         console.error(`Invalid URL: ${url}`);
78:         process.exit(1);
79:       }
80:           let parser = 'rss';
81:       for (let j = i + 2; j < args.length; j++) {
82:         if (args[j] === '--url' || args[j] === '--file') break;
83:         if (args[j] === '--parser' && j + 1 < args.length) {
84:           parser = args[j + 1];
85:           break;
86:         }
87:       }
88:       entries.push({ url, parser });
89:     }
90:   }
91: 
92:   return { entries, filePath };
93: }
94: 
95: async function loadSourcesFromYaml(filePath: string): Promise<SourceEntry[]> {
96:   const raw = await fs.readFile(filePath, 'utf-8');
97:   const data = yaml.load(raw) as any;
98: 
99:   if (!data) throw new Error(`Empty or invalid YAML file: ${filePath}`);
100: 
101:   const list: any[] = data.sources ?? (Array.isArray(data) ? data : null);
102:   if (!list) throw new Error(`YAML file must have a "sources" key or be a flat list`);
103: 
104:   return list.map((entry: any, idx: number) => {
105:     if (!entry.url) throw new Error(`Entry ${idx} is missing "url"`);
106:     if (!entry.parser) throw new Error(`Entry ${idx} is missing "parser"`);
107:     return { url: entry.url, parser: entry.parser, name: entry.name };
108:   });
109: }
110: 
111: // ── Feed type detection helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────
112: 
113: function detectFeedType(body: string, contentType: string): string {
114:   const trimmed = body.trim();
115:   if (trimmed.startsWith('<?xml') || trimmed.startsWith('<rss') || trimmed.startsWith('<feed')) {
116:     if (/<rss[\s>/]/i.test(trimmed)) return 'rss';
117:     if (/<feed[\s>/]/i.test(trimmed)) return 'atom';
118:     if (contentType.includes('xml')) return 'xml';
119:     return 'xml';
120:   }
121:   if (trimmed.startsWith('{') || trimmed.startsWith('[')) {
122:     try {
123:       const parsed = JSON.parse(trimmed);
124:       if (parsed?.version && parsed?.items) return 'json_feed';
125:       if (parsed?.items) return 'json_feed_like';
126:       return 'json';
127:     } catch {
128:       return 'json_like';
129:     }
130:   }
131:   if (trimmed.startsWith('%PDF')) return 'pdf';
132:   return 'html';
133: }
134: 
135: function getContentTypeCategory(ctype: string): string {
136:   const ct = ctype.toLowerCase();
137:   if (ct.includes('rss')) return 'application/rss+xml';
138:   if (ct.includes('atom')) return 'application/atom+xml';
139:   if (ct.includes('json')) return 'application/json';
140:   if (ct.includes('xml')) return 'application/xml';
141:   if (ct.includes('html')) return 'text/html';
142:   if (ct.includes('pdf')) return 'application/pdf';
143:   return ct.split(';')[0] || 'unknown';
144: }
145: 
146: // ── Validation per parser type ───────────────────────────────────────────────
147: 
148: async function validateSource(entry: SourceEntry): Promise<ValidationResult> {
149:   const { url, parser } = entry;
150:   const result: ValidationResult = {
151:     url,
152:     parser,
153:     status: 'fail',
154:     items_count: 0,
155:   };
156: 
157:   let body: string;
158:   let statusCode: number;
159:   let rawContentType: string;
160: 
161:   try {
162:     const controller = new AbortController();
163:     const timeout = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), 15000);
164: 
165:     const resp = await request(url, {
166:       method: 'GET',
167:       headers: {
168:         'User-Agent': 'IGS-Validator/1.0 (+https://github.com/ishan-parihar/igs-mcp)',
169:         Accept: 'application/rss+xml, application/json, text/html, application/xml, application/pdf, */*',
170:       },
171:       signal: controller.signal,
172:     });
173: 
174:     clearTimeout(timeout);
175: 
176:     statusCode = resp.statusCode;
177:     rawContentType = (resp.headers['content-type'] as string) || '';
178:     result.content_type = rawContentType;
179: 
180:     if (statusCode !== 200) {
181:       result.status = 'fail';
182:       result.error = `HTTP ${statusCode}${resp.statusMessage ? ` ${resp.statusMessage}` : ''}`;
183:       return result;
184:     }
185: 
186:     const chunks: Buffer[] = [];
187:     for await (const chunk of resp.body) {
188:       chunks.push(Buffer.isBuffer(chunk) ? chunk : Buffer.from(chunk));
189:     }
190:     body = Buffer.concat(chunks).toString('utf-8');
191: 
192:     if (body.length === 0) {
193:       result.status = 'fail';
194:       result.error = 'Empty response body';
195:       return result;
196:     }
197:   } catch (err: any) {
198:     result.status = 'fail';
199:     if (err.name === 'AbortError') {
200:       result.error = 'timeout (15s)';
201:     } else if (err.code === 'ENOTFOUND') {
202:       result.error = `DNS resolution failed: ${err.message}`;
203:     } else if (err.code === 'ECONNREFUSED') {
204:       result.error = `Connection refused: ${err.message}`;
205:     } else if (err.code === 'ECONNRESET') {
206:       result.error = `Connection reset: ${err.message}`;
207:     } else if (err.code === 'UND_ERR_HEADERS_TIMEOUT') {
208:       result.error = 'Headers timeout';
209:     } else if (err.type === 'aborted' || err.message?.includes('abort')) {
210:       result.error = 'timeout (15s)';
211:     } else {
212:       result.error = err.message?.slice(0, 200) || String(err);
213:     }
214:     return result;
215:   }
216: 
217:   result.feed_type = detectFeedType(body, rawContentType);
218: 
219:   try {
220:     await simulateParse(parser, body, result, url);
221:   } catch (parseErr: any) {
222:     result.status = 'fail';
223:     result.error = `Parse error: ${parseErr.message?.slice(0, 200) || String(parseErr)}`;
224:   }
225: 
226:   return result;
227: }
228: 
229: async function simulateParse(
230:   parser: string,
231:   body: string,
232:   result: ValidationResult,
233:   sourceUrl: string,
234: ): Promise<void> {
235:   switch (parser) {
236:     case 'rss': {
237:       let feed;
238:       try {
239:         feed = await rssParser.parseString(body);
240:       } catch {
241:         // rss-parser may also handle Atom; if it fails, try as raw XML
242:         feed = { items: [] };
243:       }
244:       const items = feed.items || [];
245:       result.items_count = items.length;
246: 
247:       if (items.length > 0) {
248:         result.status = 'ok';
249:         result.sample_title = items[0]?.title?.slice(0, 120);
250:       } else {
251:         // Check if body at least looks like valid RSS/XML
252:         if (body.includes('<item>') || body.includes('<entry>') || body.includes('<rss') || body.includes('<feed')) {
253:           result.status = 'partial';
254:           result.error = 'Feed parsed but 0 items';
255:         } else {
256:           result.status = 'fail';
257:           result.error = 'Content does not appear to be RSS/Atom XML';
258:         }
259:       }
260:       break;
261:     }
262: 
263:     case 'json_feed': {
264:       let data: any;
265:       try {
266:         data = JSON.parse(body);
267:       } catch {
268:         result.status = 'fail';
269:         result.error = 'Invalid JSON';
270:         return;
271:       }
272: 
273:       const items = Array.isArray(data?.items) ? data.items
274:         : Array.isArray(data) ? data
275:         : Array.isArray(data?.value) ? data.value
276:         : [];
277: 
278:       result.items_count = items.length;
279: 
280:       const hasVersion = typeof data?.version === 'string' || data?.version !== undefined;
281:       const hasTitle = typeof data?.title === 'string';
282: 
283:       if (items.length > 0) {
284:         result.status = 'ok';
285:         result.sample_title = items[0]?.title || items[0]?.url || '(no title)';
286:         result.sample_title = String(result.sample_title).slice(0, 120);
287:       } else if (hasVersion || hasTitle) {
288:         result.status = 'partial';
289:         result.error = 'JSON feed structure recognized but 0 items';
290:       } else if (Array.isArray(data) && data.length > 0) {
291:         // Could be an array of items (some APIs return this)
292:         result.status = 'ok';
293:         result.items_count = data.length;
294:         result.sample_title = String(data[0]?.title || data[0]?.url || '').slice(0, 120);
295:       } else {
296:         result.status = 'fail';
297:         result.error = 'JSON does not match feed structure (missing version/items)';
298:       }
299:       break;
300:     }
301: 
302:     case 'generic_html':
303:     case 'article_content': {
304:       const stripped = body.replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '').trim();
305:       if (stripped.length > 0) {
306:         result.status = 'partial';
307:         result.items_count = 0;
308:         result.feed_type = result.feed_type || 'html';
309:       } else {
310:         result.status = 'fail';
311:         result.error = 'Empty or whitespace-only HTML body';
312:       }
313:       break;
314:     }
315: 
316:     case 'sitemap': {
317:       const trimmed = body.trim();
318:       if (trimmed.includes('<urlset') || trimmed.includes('<sitemapindex')) {
319:         const locMatches = body.match(/<loc>/g);
320:         result.items_count = locMatches ? locMatches.length : 0;
321:         result.status = result.items_count > 0 ? 'ok' : 'partial';
322:         if (result.items_count === 0) result.error = 'Sitemap parsed but 0 URLs found';
323:       } else if (trimmed.startsWith('<?xml') || trimmed.startsWith('<')) {
324:         result.status = 'partial';
325:         result.items_count = 0;
326:         result.error = 'XML content but not a recognized sitemap format';
327:       } else {
328:         result.status = 'fail';
329:         result.error = 'Not a sitemap XML';
330:       }
331:       break;
332:     }
333: 
334:     case 'ofac':
335:     case 'ussf_cfc':
336:     case 'newslaundry': {
337:       const stripped = body.replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '').trim();
338:       if (stripped.length > 100) {
339:         result.status = 'partial';
340:         result.items_count = 0;
341:       } else {
342:         result.status = 'fail';
343:         result.error = `Body too short for ${parser} parser (${stripped.length} chars text)`;
344:       }
345:       break;
346:     }
347: 
348:     case 'who_dons':
349:     case 'semantic_scholar': {
350:       try {
351:         const data = JSON.parse(body);
352:         result.status = 'partial';
353:         result.items_count = Array.isArray(data) ? data.length
354:           : Array.isArray(data?.items) ? data.items.length
355:           : Array.isArray(data?.results) ? data.results.length
356:           : 0;
357:         if (result.items_count === 0) result.error = 'JSON parsed but 0 items found in expected fields';
358:       } catch {
359:         result.status = 'fail';
360:         result.error = 'Invalid JSON for parser type';
361:       }
362:       break;
363:     }
364: 
365:     case 'pdf-extractor':
366:     case 'pdf-to-markdown': {
367:       if (body.startsWith('%PDF') || body.charCodeAt(0) === 0x25) {
368:         result.status = 'partial';
369:         result.items_count = 0;
370:         result.feed_type = 'pdf';
371:       } else {
372:         result.status = 'fail';
373:         result.error = 'Not a PDF file (missing %PDF header)';
374:       }
375:       break;
376:     }
377: 
378:     default: {
379:       result.status = 'partial';
380:       result.items_count = 0;
381:       result.error = `Unknown parser type "${parser}" — only connectivity verified`;
382:       break;
383:     }
384:   }
385: }
386: 
387: // ── Report ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
388: 
389: function printReport(results: ValidationResult[]): void {
390:   const summary: Summary = { total: 0, passed: 0, failed: 0, partial: 0 };
391: 
392:   for (const r of results) {
393:     summary.total++;
394:     if (r.status === 'ok') summary.passed++;
395:     else if (r.status === 'fail') summary.failed++;
396:     else if (r.status === 'partial') summary.partial++;
397:   }
398: 
399:   const report: Report = { summary, results };
400:   console.log(JSON.stringify(report, null, 2));
401: }
402: 
403: // ── Main ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
404: 
405: async function main() {
406:   const { entries: cliEntries, filePath } = parseArgs();
407:   let allEntries: SourceEntry[] = [...cliEntries];
408: 
409:   if (filePath) {
410:     try {
411:       const fileEntries = await loadSourcesFromYaml(filePath);
412:       allEntries = allEntries.concat(fileEntries);
413:     } catch (err: any) {
414:       console.error(`Error loading YAML file "${filePath}": ${err.message}`);
415:       process.exit(1);
416:     }
417:   }
418: 
419:   if (allEntries.length === 0) {
420:     console.error('No sources to validate. Provide --url/--parser pairs or --file.');
421:     console.error('');
422:     console.error('Usage:');
423:     console.error('  bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser>');
424:     console.error('  bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --file <path-to-yaml>');
425:     process.exit(1);
426:   }
427: 
428:   const results: ValidationResult[] = [];
429: 
430:   for (let i = 0; i < allEntries.length; i++) {
431:     const entry = allEntries[i];
432:     const label = entry.name || entry.url;
433:     process.stderr.write(`[${i + 1}/${allEntries.length}] ${label}... `);
434: 
435:     const result = await validateSource(entry);
436:     results.push(result);
437: 
438:     const icons: Record<string, string> = { ok: '✓', fail: '✗', partial: '~' };
439:     process.stderr.write(`${icons[result.status] || '?'} ${result.status}`);
440:     if (result.items_count > 0) process.stderr.write(` (${result.items_count} items)`);
441:     if (result.error) process.stderr.write(` — ${result.error.slice(0, 80)}`);
442:     process.stderr.write('\n');
443:   }
444: 
445:   printReport(results);
446: 
447:   const anyFailed = results.some(r => r.status === 'fail');
448:   process.exit(anyFailed ? 1 : 0);
449: }
450: 
451: main().catch(err => {
452:   console.error('Fatal error:', err);
453:   process.exit(1);
454: });

(End of file - total 454 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
#!/usr/bin/env bun
/**
 * validate-sources.ts — Pre-validation script for RSS/JSON feeds and other sources.
 *
 * Tests each source URL for:
 *   1. HTTP 200 status
 *   2. Content-Type header inspection
 *   3. Parsing simulation (where possible)
 *
 * Usage:
 *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser>
 *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser> [--url <url2> --parser <parser2> ...]
 *   bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --file <path-to-yaml>
 *
 * YAML file format (matching sources.yml):
 *   sources:
 *     - url: https://example.com/feed
 *       parser: rss
 *       name: optional label
 *

1: # IGS Source Expansion — Learnings
2: 
3: ## Task: Add `platform`, `tier`, `rate_limit` to Source type/schema
4: 
5: ### Files Modified
6: - `src/types/news.ts` — Source type updated
7: - `src/config/schemas.ts` — SourceSchema updated
8: 
9: ### Changes Made
10: 
11: #### Source type changes:
12: - `type` expanded: `'rss' | 'http'` → `'rss' | 'http' | 'social_media'`
13: - `sourceCategory` expanded: `'news' | 'community' | 'research'` → `'news' | 'community' | 'research' | 'social'`
14: - New field: `platform?: 'reddit' | 'twitter' | 'web'`
15: - New field: `tier?: 1 | 2 | 3`
16: - New field: `rate_limit?: { interval_seconds: number; batch_size?: number }`
17: 
18: #### SourceSchema changes:
19: - `type` enum expanded similarly with z.enum
20: - `platform`: `z.enum(['reddit', 'twitter', 'web']).optional()`
21: - `tier`: `z.union([z.literal(1), z.literal(2), z.literal(3)]).optional()`
22: - `rate_limit`: `z.object({ interval_seconds: z.number().int().positive(), batch_size: z.number().int().positive().optional() }).optional()`
23: - `sourceCategory` enum expanded + kept `optional().default('news')`
24: 
25: ### Key Decisions
26: - All new fields are OPTIONAL — backward compatible with existing sources.yml
27: - `platform` defaults to undefined (backward compat implied as 'web' at usage time)
28: - `tier` defaults to undefined (implied Tier 2 at usage time)
29: - `rate_limit` entirely optional — no rate limiting unless explicitly configured
30: - Placed new fields between `is_active` and `sourceCategory` in both type and schema
31: 
32: ### Build Result
33: - `bun run build` passes with zero errors
34: 
35: ---
36: 
37: ## Task: Add 4 new pools to pools.yml + split GLOBAL_ENV_HEALTH
38: 
39: ### Files Modified
40: - `config/pools.yml` — pool definitions updated
41: 
42: ### Changes Made
43: - **Deactivated** `GLOBAL_ENV_HEALTH` (set `is_active: false`) — not removed
44: - **Added 4 new pools** at end of file:
45:   - `GLOBAL_HEALTH` — Pandemics, disease outbreaks, health policy, biosecurity
46:   - `GLOBAL_ENVIRONMENT` — Climate change, biodiversity, energy transition
47:   - `GLOBAL_SCIENCE` — Scientific research, space exploration, discovery
48:   - `GLOBAL_DEFENSE_SECURITY` — Military, defense, aerospace, arms control
49: - `GLOBAL_CULT_SOC` left untouched (already existed, already active)
50: 
51: ### Key Decisions
52: - Deactivated rather than deleted `GLOBAL_ENV_HEALTH` for audit trail
53: - Section header comments follow existing YAML style
54: - YAML validated with Python `yaml.safe_load()`
55: 
56: ---
57: 
58: ## Task: Create centralized rate limiter at `src/utils/rate-limiter.ts`
59: 
60: ### Files Created
61: - `src/utils/rate-limiter.ts` — New file
62: 
63: ### Changes Made
64: 
65: #### RateLimiter class (exported):
66: - `setLimit(key, config)` — Register rate limit config
67: - `check(key)` — Non-mutating check if request is allowed (prunes + checks count)
68: - `wait(key, timeoutMs?)` — Polls every 100ms until slot available or timeout (30s default), records timestamp on success, throws `RateLimitError` on timeout
69: - `remaining(key)` — Remaining slots in current window, `Infinity` if no config
70: - `timeUntilNext(key)` — MS until next slot, 0 if room available or no config
71: - `reset(key)` — Clears timestamps and config for key
72: 
73: #### Named exports:
74: - `RateLimitConfig` interface
75: - `RateLimitError` class (extends Error, has `key` and `retryAfterMs` fields)
76: - `REDDIT_PLATFORM_LIMIT` — 60 req / 60s
77: - `TWITTER_PLATFORM_LIMIT` — 450 req / 15min
78: - `DEFAULT_SOURCE_INTERVAL_MS` — 300000 (5 min)
79: - `sourceRateLimitKey(sourceId)` — Returns `source:${sourceId}`
80: - `platformRateLimitKey(platform)` — Returns `platform:${platform}`
81: 
82: ### Implementation Details
83: - Sliding window: array of timestamps per key, pruned via binary search (`lowerBound`)
84: - No external dependencies — pure in-memory Map storage
85: - `lowerBound` helper function for efficient O(log n) pruning
86: - Default timeout for `wait()` is 30s, polling interval 100ms
87: - JS single-threaded — no locks needed for concurrency
88: 
89: ### Key Decisions
90: - `check()` does NOT record requests (read-only), `wait()` records after acquiring slot
91: - Config stored as spread copy (`{ ...config }`) to prevent external mutation
92: - No limit configured = pass-through (returns true / Infinity / 0)
93: - Single file with no imports from other packages for zero-dependency isolation
94: 
95: ### Build Result
96: - `bun run build` passes with zero errors
97: - Zero LSP diagnostics on new file

(End of file - total 97 lines)
</content>

input.newString:
### Build Result
- `bun run build` passes with zero errors
- Zero LSP diagnostics on new file

---

## Task: Create source validation script at `scripts/validate-sources.ts`

### Files Created
- `scripts/validate-sources.ts` — New standalone validation script

### Usage
```
bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser>
bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --file <path-to-yaml>
```

### CLI Interface
- `--url <url> --parser <parser>` — Single source pair; can be repeated for multiple sources
- `--file <path>` — YAML file with sources (supports both `sources:` key wrapper and flat list format)
- Exit code 0 if all pass, 1 if any fail

### Validation logic per parser type:
- **rss**: HTTP fetch → `rss-parser.parseString()` → items > 0 = ok, 0 = partial, parse failure = fail
- **json_feed**: HTTP fetch → JSON.parse → check `version` + `items` fields
- **generic_html / article_content**: HTTP 200 + non-empty stripped body → partial
- **sitemap**: Check for `<urlset>` or `<sitemapindex>` XML structure → count `<loc>` entries
- **ofac / ussf_cfc / newslaundry**: Custom HTML → non-empty body > 100 chars stripped → partial
- **who_dons / semantic_scholar**: Custom JSON → verify parseable, count items/results → partial
- **pdf-extractor / pdf-to-markdown**: Check for `%PDF` magic bytes → partial

### Output format:
```json
{
  "summary": { "total": 3, "passed": 2, "failed": 0, "partial": 1 },
  "results": [
    { "url": "...", "parser": "rss", "status": "ok", "items_count": 40, "content_type": "...", "feed_type": "rss", "sample_title": "..." }
  ]
}
```

### Key Decisions
- Standalone script — no imports from `src/` to keep it portable and testable
- Uses existing deps: `rss-parser`, `undici`, `js-yaml`
- Unknown parser types get `partial` status with connectivity verification only
- Progress output on stderr, JSON report on stdout (pipeline-friendly)
- Feed type auto-detected from body content (rss/atom/json/html/pdf)
- Network errors mapped to readable messages (DNS, timeout, connection refused, connection reset)

### Verification
- Tested with BBC RSS (40 items ✓), Daring Fireball Atom (48 items ✓), example.com HTML (~ partial), httpstat.us/404 (✗)

+- `scripts/validate-sources.ts` — New standalone validation script
+
+### Usage
+```
+bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --url <url> --parser <parser>
+bun run scripts/validate-sources.ts --file <path-to-yaml>
+```
+
+### CLI Interface
+- `--url <url> --parser <parser>` — Single source pair; can be repeated for multiple sources
+- `--file <path>` — YAML file with sources (supports both `sources:` key wrapper and flat list format)
+- Exit code 0 if all pass, 1 if any fail
+
+### Validation logic per parser type:
+- **rss**: HTTP fetch → `rss-parser.parseString()` → items > 0 = ok, 0 = partial, parse failure = fail
+- **json_feed**: HTTP fetch → JSON.parse → check `version` + `items` fields
+- **generic_html / article_content**: HTTP 200 + non-empty stripped body → partial
+- **sitemap**: Check for `<urlset>` or `<sitemapindex>` XML structure → count `<loc>` entries
+- **ofac / ussf_cfc / newslaundry**: Custom HTML → non-empty body > 100 chars stripped → partial
+- **who_dons / semantic_scholar**: Custom JSON → verify parseable, count items/results → partial
+- **pdf-extractor / pdf-to-markdown**: Check for `%PDF` magic bytes → partial
+
+### Output format:
+```json
+{
+  "summary": { "total": 3, "passed": 2, "failed": 0, "partial": 1 },
+  "results": [
+    { "url": "...", "parser": "rss", "status": "ok", "items_count": 40, "content_type": "...", "feed_type": "rss", "sample_title": "..." }
+  ]
+}
+```
+
+### Key Decisions
+- Standalone script — no imports from `src/` to keep it portable and testable
+- Uses existing deps: `rss-parser`, `undici`, `js-yaml`
+- Unknown parser types get `partial` status with connectivity verification only
+- Progress output on stderr, JSON report on stdout (pipeline-friendly)
+- Feed type auto-detected from body content (rss/atom/json/html/pdf)
+- Network errors mapped to readable messages (DNS, timeout, connection refused, connection reset)
+
+### Verification
+- Tested with BBC RSS (40 items ✓), Daring Fireball Atom (48 items ✓), example.com HTML (~ partial), httpstat.us/404 (✗)
